steps.title: Find the key relationship in the notes
steps.content: The notes show a cause-and-effect pattern: as wind conditions worsened (still $\Rightarrow$ strong breeze $\Rightarrow$ near-gale), auk landing success dropped (nearly 100% $\Rightarrow$ ~40% $\Rightarrow$ ~20%). An effective summary must capture this relationship, not just describe the study's setup or goals.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• "For a 2019 study, researchers from the UK and Germany collected data on auks' attempts to land at cliffside nesting sites in different wind conditions.": describes only the methodology, not the finding — the result is never stated. ✗
  • "Emily Shepard and her colleagues wanted to know the extent to which wind affected auks' success in landing at cliffside nesting sites, so they conducted a study.": states only the researchers’ aim, not what they found. ✗
  • "Knowing that auks often need multiple attempts to land at their cliffside nesting sites, Emily Shepard studied the birds' success rate, which was only around 20% in some conditions.": cites one data point but omits the key factor (wind) driving the change. ✗
  • "Emily Shepard's 2019 study of auks' success in landing at cliffside nesting sites showed that as wind conditions intensified, the birds' success rate decreased.": captures the who, when, and core cause-and-effect finding of the study. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
